name: finance-os
description: Personal finance app: FastAPI backend, Vite + React frontend, SQLite storage. Designed to run locally via Docker Compose or Forger.

changes: Bumps Finance OS version to exercise the Forger app update flow.

changelog.summary: Adds localized onboarding, budgets, category management, and faster assisted imports.
changes: Adds a first-run onboarding flow with selectable category presets and review-first import guidance.
Adds localized Finance OS UI copy, assistant progress language handling, and AI subscription gating.
Adds budget period management and recommendation flows backed by MCP tools.
Improves assisted imports with local preprocessing, batch MCP writes, stronger duplicate tracking, and review warnings.
Publishes the Finance OS catalog icon through the release workflow.

changelog.summary: Improves FORGER Cloud release publishing diagnostics.
changes: Adds a dedicated publisher user agent for FORGER Cloud release notifications.
Reports HTTP response details when automatic release publishing fails.

changelog.summary: Fixes movement reclassification and publishes releases to FORGER Cloud.
changes: Fixes category updates when choosing a subcategory from another category.
Adds verification coverage for cross-category subcategory edits.
Publishes release metadata directly to FORGER Cloud with a signed request.

changelog.summary: Aligns backend packaging with the shared stack source layout.
changes: Moves the backend app package under backend/src for the current stack contract.
Updates service, MCP, and internal script commands to run with the src-based Python path.
Keeps Finance OS install and agent operations compatible with current Forger Desktop app runtime expectations.

changelog.summary: Marks the app as beta in Forger catalog surfaces.
changes: Adds beta metadata so Forger can label the app in catalog and installed-app cards.

changelog.summary: Adds structured MCP tools for Forger agents.
changes: Declares the Finance OS MCP server in the app manifest.
Adds structured MCP tools for categories, subcategories, movements, movement edits and deletes, and CSV imports.
Keeps Finance OS data operations behind app validations instead of direct database manipulation.

changelog.summary: Adds assistant-assisted statement imports through Forger Desktop.
changes: Routes PDF and image statement imports through Forger Desktop assistant tasks.
Declares typed prompt arguments for assistant document processing.
Shows clearer import progress and markdown-formatted assistant results.
Adds PDF text extraction support for agent-assisted imports.
Removes in-app OpenAI API key settings from the import flow.

changelog.summary: Hardens release packaging to exclude Git metadata.
changes: Excludes root and nested .git metadata from app release packages.
Adds a defensive cleanup pass before creating the release ZIP.

changelog.summary: Improves AI-assisted imports, classification consistency, and review workflows.
changes: Adds AI-assisted import and review improvements for statements and movement files.
Keeps stored movement amounts as positive magnitudes while category kind represents direction.
Enforces category and subcategory consistency across imports, edits, and verification.
Adds local data integrity verification and supporting agent guidance.
Updates Finance OS agent guidance in English with clearer user-visible capability boundaries.
Splits post-install guidance into English and Spanish documents.
Updates README documentation to match the current local app and release flow.

author: FinanceOS

catalog.short_description: Personal finance tracker with AI assistant. Your data, your machine.
catalog.description: Finance OS is a local-first personal finance app. Track income and expenses, categorize movements, import bank statements, and ask the assistant questions about your finances. Your database stays on your machine; PDFs and images you choose to process with the assistant are handled through Forger Desktop.

capabilities.title: Guardar tus finanzas localmente
capabilities.description: Mantiene movimientos, categorias y presupuestos en una base de datos local.

capabilities.title: Importar archivos que eliges
capabilities.description: Carga cartolas o planillas seleccionadas por ti para convertirlas en movimientos.

capabilities.title: Usar el asistente para leer documentos
capabilities.description: Procesa PDFs e imagenes mediante el asistente provisto por Forger, sin API key propia dentro de la app.

promptTemplates.title: First run: discover categories and load movements
promptTemplates.description: Reads initial financial files, creates starter categories, imports movements, and prepares review.
promptTemplates.prompt:
You are the internal Finance OS agent.

Goal:
This is the user's first import. The user already chose a starter category style in Finance OS, and the app created that category/subcategory schema before this task started. Read the documents, adapt movement classifications to the existing schema, create only clearly useful missing subcategories or categories with Finance OS MCP, import the movements in one structured batch, and leave them pending review.

Files to process:
{{statement}}

Local preprocessing extracted from those files, when available:
{{preprocessedDocuments}}

User note with selected category preset and existing schema:
{{userNote}}

Response language:
{{locale}}

Instructions:
1. Write every progress message and the final answer in the requested language. If the language is es, use Spanish. If it is en, use English.
2. Use the local preprocessing text/tables first because it is faster and more reliable. Inspect the original files only when the preprocessed content is missing, incomplete, or ambiguous.
3. Extract only visible movements. Files may be CSV, XLSX, PDF, or images. Do not invent rows, dates, amounts, merchants, or descriptions.
4. Read the existing category schema from the user note and preserve it. Prefer classifying into those categories/subcategories instead of replacing the structure.
5. Create missing subcategories only when the document shows a clear repeated pattern that does not fit an existing subcategory. Create a missing top-level category only when the current schema cannot represent the movement type.
6. Use English canonical category and subcategory names when creating new categories or subcategories, regardless of the document language.
7. Classify deposits, salary, refunds, or other real income into the existing income category/subcategory when available.
8. Detect internal transfers, payments between the user's own accounts, credit card payments, account transfers, adjustments, and movements that do not represent real expense or income. The category must be Non accountable and the specific type must be the subcategory: Internal transfers, Credit card payment, Transfers between accounts, Adjustments, or another English subcategory if the document shows a clear pattern. If that subcategory is missing, include it in the category batch before classifying movements.
9. Avoid redundant categories. Prefer the existing schema and clear subcategories.
10. Use MCP for all writes. Do not write directly to the database and do not use ad hoc scripts.
11. First call ensure_category_tree once with every missing category/subcategory needed. Use the returned IDs or names for classification. Do not create categories or subcategories one by one unless the batch tool is unavailable.
12. Then call import_movements once with structured JSON. Do not use import_csv_text unless import_movements is unavailable. Include source_label, source_file, source_row when visible, date, accounting_date when visible, amount, business, reason, raw_description, source, reviewed=false, and category/subcategory IDs or names.
13. Preserve the original description, accounting date, source file, and source type.
14. If a classification is unclear, choose the closest existing category, leave subcategory empty when needed, and keep reviewed=false so the user can correct it in review.
15. Keep this rule: if a movement has a subcategory, it belongs to the same category as that subcategory.
16. Run verify_data_integrity after importing.
17. Report inserted, duplicate, and failed counts separately from the import result. A duplicate is not a failed import.
18. Do not expose paths, commands, or technical details.

Final answer:
Explain in functional language which categories/subcategories were used or added, how many movements were inserted, how many were duplicates, how many failed, which movements were marked as Non accountable or internal transfers, what remains pending review, and that the user can review everything in the next view.

promptTemplates.title: Load more movements
promptTemplates.description: Reads additional financial files, preserves the current structure, and proposes missing categories when useful.
promptTemplates.prompt:
You are the internal Finance OS agent.

Goal:
Read the financial documents shared by the user, preserve their current category/subcategory structure, propose new categories only when needed, and import the movements into Finance OS.

Files to process:
{{statement}}

Local preprocessing extracted from those files, when available:
{{preprocessedDocuments}}

User note:
{{userNote}}

Response language:
{{locale}}

Instructions:
1. Write every progress message and the final answer in the requested language. If the language is es, use Spanish. If it is en, use English.
2. Use the local preprocessing text/tables first because it is faster and more reliable. Inspect the original files only when the preprocessed content is missing, incomplete, or ambiguous.
3. Review existing categories, subcategories, and movements with MCP before classifying.
4. Extract only visible movements. Do not invent data.
5. Preserve the user's current structure whenever it is reasonable. If no structure exists yet, organize expenses into Essentials, Non-essential, and Extraordinary, with subcategories detected from the movements.
6. Use Income for deposits, salary, refunds, or other real income.
7. Detect internal transfers, payments between the user's own accounts, credit card payments, account transfers, adjustments, and movements that do not represent real expense or income. The category must be exactly Non accountable and the specific type must be the subcategory: Internal transfers, Credit card payment, Transfers between accounts, Adjustments, or another detected subcategory if the document shows a clear pattern. If that subcategory is missing, include it in the category batch before classifying movements.
8. Use MCP for all writes. Do not write directly to the database and do not use ad hoc scripts.
9. First call ensure_category_tree once for any missing categories/subcategories. If nothing is missing, skip that write. Do not create categories or subcategories one by one unless the batch tool is unavailable.
10. Then call import_movements once with structured JSON. Do not use import_csv_text unless import_movements is unavailable. Include source_label, source_file, source_row when visible, date, accounting_date when visible, amount, business, reason, raw_description, source, reviewed=false, and category/subcategory IDs or names.
11. Avoid duplicates within the batch when you are certain they are the same source row. If two rows share date, amount, and description but appear as separate visible rows, import both; Finance OS will flag the later one for review as a possible duplicate. If the import reports duplicates, summarize them as duplicates, not failures.
12. Leave uncertain classifications pending review.
13. Run verify_data_integrity after importing.
14. Report inserted, duplicate, and failed counts separately from the import result.
15. Do not expose paths, commands, or technical details.

Final answer:
Summarize how many movements were inserted, how many were duplicates, how many failed, which new categories were recommended or created, how many movements were marked as Non accountable or internal transfers, what remains pending review, and any concrete issue.

promptTemplates.title: Recommend budget
promptTemplates.description: Recommends category/subcategory budgets for a period using expected income and local history.
promptTemplates.prompt:
You are the internal Finance OS agent.

Goal:
Recommend and load budgets for period {{month}}/{{year}} using expected income {{expectedIncome}}, existing categories, and historical movements.

Response language:
{{locale}}

Instructions:
1. Write every progress message and the final answer in the requested language. If the language is es, use Spanish. If it is en, use English.
2. Review existing categories, subcategories, movements, and budgets with MCP.
3. Create or update the period budget with MCP.
4. Create CategoryBudget and SubcategoryBudget rows with MCP according to the recommendation.
5. Do not validate that totals match the income; use income only as context for the recommendation.
6. Do not expose paths, commands, or technical details.

Final answer:
Summarize the recommended budget and the main allocations created or updated.

scripts.init_db: cd backend && PYTHONPATH=./src uv run python scripts/init_db.py
scripts.list_categories: cd backend && PYTHONPATH=./src uv run python scripts/list_categories.py
scripts.list_movements: cd backend && PYTHONPATH=./src uv run python scripts/list_movements.py
scripts.import_movements: cd backend && PYTHONPATH=./src uv run python scripts/import_movements.py
scripts.upsert_categories: cd backend && PYTHONPATH=./src uv run python scripts/upsert_categories.py
scripts.edit_movement: cd backend && PYTHONPATH=./src uv run python scripts/edit_movement.py
scripts.delete_movement: cd backend && PYTHONPATH=./src uv run python scripts/delete_movement.py
scripts.verify_data_integrity: cd backend && PYTHONPATH=./src uv run python scripts/verify_data_integrity.py
